name: Running circular at Embalse del Sitjar
name_original: Running circular al Embalse del Sitjar
region_intro: Jogging routes around Embalse de Sichar offer a diverse landscape characterized by crystalline waters and surrounding green expanses. The region features pine forests providing natural shade and a refreshing environment for runners. Trails often follow the Mijares River basin, contributing to the area's ecological richness. Panoramic viewpoints along many routes provide scenic breaks and motivational spots for runners. Best jogging routes around Embalse de Sichar The most popular jogging route is Running circular at Embalse del Sitjar, a 13.4 miles (21.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive views of the reservoir and its natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local runners is Ribesalbes to Fanzara Route – Old Path to Fanzara loop from Ribesalbes, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route connects two towns through varied terrain, including sections along the river. Local runners also love the Sitjar Dam loop from Ribesalbes, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail leading through the reservoir's edge and surrounding hills, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Jogging around Embalse de Sichar is defined by crystalline waters, pine forests, and panoramic viewpoints. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Embalse de Sichar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 25 reviews. More than 1500 runners have used komoot to explore Embalse de Sichar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Embalse de Sichar?
A: There are over 100 running routes available around Embalse de Sichar, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there are also easier paths around Embalse de Sichar. These routes often feature accessible terrain and provide a refreshing environment for a leisurely run or a family outing. Look for routes with lower elevation gain for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails around Embalse de Sichar?
A: The terrain around Embalse de Sichar is quite varied. You can expect paths through pine forests, along the crystalline waters of the reservoir, and sections that follow the Mijares River basin. Some routes may include varied terrain connecting towns, while others lead through surrounding hills, offering a mix of surfaces from dirt paths to more rugged trails.

Q: Are there circular running routes around Embalse de Sichar?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Embalse de Sichar are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880757176/running-circular-at-embalse-del-sitjar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Embalse del Sitjar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers extensive views of the reservoir. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23832646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sitjar Dam loop from Ribesalbes&lt;/a&gt;, leading through the reservoir's edge and surrounding hills.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or natural features to see while running?
A: The Embalse de Sichar region is known for its stunning natural beauty. Many trails offer panoramic views of the reservoir and its crystalline waters. You'll run through lush green expanses and pine forests, enjoying the contrast between the blue water and green vegetation. The Mijares River basin also contributes to the ecological richness, with opportunities to spot local fauna.

Q: Is there parking available near the running routes?
A: Yes, the area around Embalse de Sichar is generally accessible, and many starting points for running routes have parking facilities. The ability to 'go around it by van' suggests accessible perimeter roads or paths where parking can often be found, making it convenient to start your run.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail may vary, the natural and tranquil environment of Embalse de Sichar is generally welcoming for outdoor activities with pets. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural beauty for everyone.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Embalse de Sichar?
A: The running routes in Embalse de Sichar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 25 reviews. Runners often praise the serene and picturesque environment, the clear waters, and the natural shade provided by the pine forests. The extensive trail network and amazing views throughout also contribute to an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any cafes or amenities near the running trails?
A: The area features well-equipped recreational zones with tables, benches, and sometimes barbecue facilities, perfect for post-activity relaxation. While specific cafes directly on the trails might be limited, the nearby town of Onda offers additional amenities, including cafes and restaurants, to complement your outdoor experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running around Embalse de Sichar?
A: Embalse de Sichar offers a refreshing environment year-round. The natural shade from pine forests makes it pleasant even on warmer days. For comfortable running, spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures. However, the tranquil atmosphere and beautiful scenery can be enjoyed in any season.

Q: How long do the running trails typically take to complete?
A: The duration of running trails around Embalse de Sichar varies significantly depending on the route's distance and difficulty. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880757176/running-circular-at-embalse-del-sitjar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Embalse del Sitjar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.4 miles (21.5 km), typically takes about 2 hours 26 minutes. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14851713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribesalbes to Fanzara Route&lt;/a&gt; (6.1 miles / 9.8 km) can be completed in around 1 hour 11 minutes.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions nearby?
A: Yes, the nearby town of Onda, which is close to Embalse de Sichar, features a historical castle. This provides an additional cultural attraction for visitors, complementing the natural appeal of the reservoir and offering a chance to explore local heritage after your run.
tips: Built in the 1960s, the reservoir is one of the most picturesque natural settings in the province of Castellón and offers a sensation of tranquility like few others. It has several beaches and recreational areas with tables and grills and where you can also take a relaxing bath submerging yourself in its crystal clear waters. Remember that fishing and boating are not allowed.
